title: 第 89 節
en: — (Surya, lektra\ her bridegroom, — in Lettish mythology, 433, 594. Sun-tree in Finnish mythology, 438, 438 n. .Suna, 57.
Sundi-Mumi, Sun-Mother, 466. Suonetar, Finnish goddess of muscles and veins, 273. Superstitions, modern, 189. Surya, m., the Sun, 39, 45, 56, 171.
— not restricted to one phenomenon, — superseded by Agni, 142.
— represents all phenomena in the sky, 478.
Surya, son of Heaven and Earth, 496.
— a white horse, 653. Surya, fem., Sun, 39, 47.
— the Sun-daughter, Lettish parallel to, 434. Sut, god of darkness, 166.
— devoured the head of Osiris, or the eye of Horus, 167. Suvetar, the same as Etelatar, 269. Svar, gen. suras, sun and sky, 172.

— sun, the shiner, 208. Svar-bhanu, demon causing eclipses of the sun, 654. Syama, the dark, the night, 628, 630. Syncretism and Allelotheism, 146. Syria, carried off by Pourgas to be his wife, 244, 246.
— the Dawn, Mordvinian goddess, Syriava, story of Pourgas and, 244.
TADEBEJO, Finnish name for ancestral spirits, 274.

Tadebejos, spirits of the Samoyedes, Tilhti, star or pole star, Finnish god, — sons and daughters of, 266. Taivahan Ukko, or Father of the Sky, Talatala and Tartaros, 669. Talbot, Fox, on Dian-nisi, 218. Talmud, Relig. Ceremonies in, 694 n. Tanunapat, 57.
— a name of Agni, 788. Tapas, meanings of, 221, 223.
— and Tapasvin, modern and ancient authorities on, 223.
